summary: Iteration 2 of design-writer/output/design.md resolves all three iteration-1 findings and is fully aligned with both guides. F1 (button sizing): every paused-menu button is now `px-3 py-2 text-sm font-semibold`, matching ui-style-guide 'Buttons' and the sibling Approve/Send-answers submits in the same partial. F2 (disable-while-submitting): Explore, Clarifying Questions, Repeat from the Beginning, and Done all carry `data-turbo-submits-with`; 'Ask Human' is an <a> anchor (a scroll, not a submit) so correctly has none. F3 (PORO placement): `Phases::Convergence` is relocated to app/queries/phases/convergence.rb and explicitly labeled a read-only query-object predicate per backend-guide 'Query objects' (app/queries/ confirmed present in the repo). Backend alignment verified against repo precedent: services are verb-first `self.call` -> Result with keyword args (Result.failure(:sym, record:) matches app/services/result.rb exactly), transactions are service-owned, and broadcasts fire after commit (RestartDefine broadcasts after its transaction block; ManagerTick via broadcast_affected). Controllers stay thin -- pause/rerun_step/restart mirror the existing send_back/answers PRG pattern with private *_alert response helpers and reuse membership_scoped_phase; models gain only the `paused` enum value and the small `any_step_active?` predicate, with no business-logic callbacks; migration uses reversible add_column with null:false defaults; Minitest coverage is listed for every new service, query, controller, model, and helper. UI alignment verified: `paused` maps to the amber 'attention' tone in the single shared STATUS_TONES map -- exactly what the ui-style-guide status table prescribes for 'paused phases' -- status_badge always renders the label (never color alone), and every progress message uses aria-live="polite". One non-blocking observation (not a finding): 'Repeat from the Beginning' is destructive and styled as a neutral secondary; the guide reserves red for destructive actions and requires a confirm (present) but does not mandate red on every confirmed action, and the design's single-primary (Done)/rest-secondary treatment is coherent and restrained -- worth a glance but not a deviation.
